shows.title: AI & Robots / ET Contact Experiences
shows.first_half: First Half: Professor Anne Foerst will discuss how AI has evolved from earlier approaches to today’s large language models. She'll focus on a key difference: earlier robotics were embodied systems that learned through interaction with the world, while modern AI is largely disembodied, trained on language patterns without true intelligence.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Writer Damien Kross had a "nightshift" experience in which a craft stopped moving across the sky after he shone a flashlight toward its location. This led him to further research into the subject of extraterrestrials and multiple ongoing ET contact experiences.

shows.title: Rock Music & CREEM Magazine / Open Lines
shows.first_half: Jaan Uhelszki is an American music journalist and co-founder of CREEM magazine. She joins guest host Rich Berra (email) to discuss the mythic rise, spectacular fall, and unlikely resurrection of CREEM, called "America’s Only Rock ’n’ Roll Magazine." She'll talk about the writers who became legends, the thousands of interviews that captured rock at its most dangerous, and how CREEM both fueled and exposed one of music’s most infamous conspiracy theories. Followed by Open Lines. Give Rich a call and discuss whatever is on your mind!
